title: entandrophragma angolense var. macrophyllum
intro: Entandrophragma angolense var. macrophyllum, also known as the Angolan mahogany or African mahogany, is a tree species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Entandrophragma angolense var. macrophyllum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
